documents.title: Documents
documents.subtitle: Manage CRA compliance documentation for this product.

declaration_of_conformity.title: Declaration of Conformity
declaration_of_conformity.description: EU Declaration of Conformity per CRA Annex V. Required for placing your product on the EU market.

vulnerability_disclosure_policy.title: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy
vulnerability_disclosure_policy.description: Coordinated vulnerability disclosure process. Required under CRA Article 11.

incident_report.title: Incident Report Template
incident_report.description: Security incident notification template per CRA Article 14. For reporting to ENISA.

risk_assessment.title: Risk Assessment Matrix
risk_assessment.description: Cybersecurity risk assessment. Identifies threats, vulnerabilities, and mitigation measures.

technical_documentation.title: Technical Documentation
technical_documentation.description: Technical documentation outline per CRA Annex VII. Covers design, development, and testing.

manufacturerName.title: Manufacturer Name
manufacturerName.body: The legal entity that places the product on the EU market. Must match your company registration name exactly — buyers and notified bodies cross-check this against trade-register filings.

manufacturerAddress.title: Manufacturer Address
manufacturerAddress.body: Your registered business address, including postal code and country. Use the address on file with your tax authority — not a shipping or marketing address.

productName.title: Product Name
productName.body: The commercial name buyers see — what's printed on the packaging and in the product listing. Match it exactly to the name used in marketing collateral.

productIdentification.title: Product Identification
productIdentification.body: A unique identifier that distinguishes this product from other variants you ship. Could be a model number, SKU, GTIN, or hardware serial range. Buyers must be able to trace any specific unit back to this declaration.

conformityStatement.title: Conformity Statement
conformityStatement.body: The legal sentence asserting the product meets the CRA's essential cybersecurity requirements. The default boilerplate is the wording recommended in Annex IV — leave it unless legal counsel has approved a variant.

standardsApplied.title: Standards Applied
standardsApplied.body: Comma-separated list of the harmonised standards or technical specifications you've used to demonstrate conformity. Common examples: IEC 62443-4-2 for industrial IoT, ETSI EN 303 645 for consumer IoT, ISO/IEC 27034 for application security.

notifiedBodyName.title: Notified Body Name
notifiedBodyName.body: Required only when your conformity route involves a notified body (Module B+C, H, or European cybersecurity certification). Leave blank if you've self-assessed under Module A.

notifiedBodyNumber.title: Notified Body Number
notifiedBodyNumber.body: The four-digit number assigned to the notified body by the European Commission (e.g. 0035 for TÜV Rheinland LGA). Required whenever Notified Body Name is filled.

place.title: Place of Issue
place.body: The city and country where the declaration is signed. Usually your company HQ.

date.title: Date of Issue
date.body: The date you sign the declaration. Must be on or before the date the product is first placed on the EU market.

signatoryName.title: Signatory Name
signatoryName.body: Full name of the person legally authorised by the manufacturer to sign the declaration. Typically a director, CTO, or compliance officer with formal authority.

signatoryPosition.title: Signatory Position
signatoryPosition.body: The signer's job title within the manufacturer's legal entity (e.g. Chief Technology Officer, Compliance Director). Reinforces that they have authority to bind the company.

policyScope.title: Policy Scope
policyScope.body: The products, versions, and services this policy covers. Be specific — buyers and researchers should be able to tell at a glance whether their finding falls under the policy.

reportingChannels.title: Reporting Channels
reportingChannels.body: Where researchers should send vulnerability reports. List at least one secure channel (email + PGP key, encrypted form, or VDP platform). RFC 9116 (security.txt) is the recommended discovery format.

responseTimeline.title: Response Timeline
responseTimeline.body: The maximum time between receiving a report and acknowledging it to the researcher. Industry baseline is 24–72 hours for initial acknowledgement, 5–10 business days for triage and severity assessment.

disclosureTimeline.title: Disclosure Timeline
disclosureTimeline.body: When and how you'll publish a CVE or advisory once a fix lands. The CRA pushes for coordinated disclosure aligned with the patch release; typical industry practice is 90 days from initial report.

safeHarborStatement.title: Safe Harbor Statement
safeHarborStatement.body: Assurance that good-faith security researchers will not face legal action or contractual penalties for finding and reporting vulnerabilities within the policy scope. Standard practice references CFAA / Computer Misuse Act safe-harbor language.

incidentTitle.title: Incident Title
incidentTitle.body: Short identifier for the incident — what you'll use in internal comms and ENISA submissions. e.g. 'INC-2026-007 — Auth bypass in firmware 2.1.4'.

incidentDate.title: Incident Date
incidentDate.body: When the incident actually happened, not when you discovered it. If unknown, use your best estimate and note it in the description.

incidentDescription.title: Incident Description
incidentDescription.body: Plain-English summary of what happened, what asset was affected, and how it was detected. Aim for 3-5 sentences — detail goes in the impact assessment.

impactAssessment.title: Impact Assessment
impactAssessment.body: The actual or expected consequences. Include the number of users / devices affected, data exposed, downtime, and any safety implications. ENISA expects severity to drive the reporting clock.

mitigationActions.title: Mitigation Actions
mitigationActions.body: What you've done and what you plan to do — short-term containment, long-term remediation, and customer guidance. The CRA's 72-hour intermediate report expects a credible mitigation plan.

notificationDate.title: Notification Date
notificationDate.body: When you notified the CSIRT / ENISA. The CRA clock requires early warning within 24 hours of becoming aware, intermediate report within 72 hours, and final report within one month.

riskTitle.title: Risk Title
riskTitle.body: Short label for this risk — what you'd call it in a status report. e.g. 'RA-04 — Firmware integrity bypass via OTA channel'.

threatDescription.title: Threat Description
threatDescription.body: What an attacker is trying to achieve and what capability they need. Cover the threat actor, attack vector, and goal. Reference STRIDE or LINDDUN categories if you've adopted a threat-model framework.

vulnerabilitiesIdentified.title: Vulnerabilities Identified
vulnerabilitiesIdentified.body: The weaknesses in your product that the threat could exploit. Be concrete — link to specific code paths, design flaws, configuration defaults, or third-party components.

riskLevel.title: Risk Level
riskLevel.body: Combined likelihood × impact rating after considering existing controls. Most CRA-aligned frameworks use Low / Medium / High / Critical bands; align with your company's risk-management policy if it exists.

mitigationPlan.title: Mitigation Plan
mitigationPlan.body: The controls you're adding or strengthening to reduce the risk to an acceptable level. Include who owns each action, what 'done' looks like, and the target completion date.

residualRisk.title: Residual Risk
residualRisk.body: The risk that remains after mitigations are applied. Document why this level is acceptable and which executive accepted it — auditors will ask for both.

techDocScope.title: Documentation Scope
techDocScope.body: What this technical file covers — usually a single product family or hardware platform. State it explicitly so a notified body can scope their assessment.

designDescription.title: Design Description
designDescription.body: High-level architecture: components, data flows, trust boundaries, and the security-relevant design choices. Diagrams beat prose — paste them in or link to an architecture deck.

developmentProcess.title: Development Process
developmentProcess.body: How you ship secure code: SDLC stages, code review policy, static / dynamic analysis tools, dependency scanning, and security gating at each milestone. Reference your internal policy doc if you have one.

testingResults.title: Testing & Verification
testingResults.body: Summary of penetration tests, fuzzing campaigns, conformance assessments, and vulnerability scans. Link to or attach the full reports; this section is the executive summary.

updateMechanism.title: Update Mechanism
updateMechanism.body: How you deliver security updates to deployed products: OTA, app store, manual download, etc. The CRA mandates that updates be available throughout the product's support period.

supportPeriod.title: Support Period
supportPeriod.body: The window during which you'll provide free security updates. CRA Article 13 requires a minimum of 5 years, or the expected product lifetime if longer.
